preset_description: Dark = identity signal. Visual language expresses who the user is. Validator gets authority and weight (heavy dark). Mover gets minimal light with one dark CTA punch. Explorer gets all-neutral — open, spacious, no urgency.

brief.audience: Startups seeking an ai-first, intent-driven design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Establishes immediate context for startup founders exploring an AI-first, intent-driven design system. Sets the frame for what MODE is and who it serves, anchoring the Explorer archetype's need to understand before engaging further.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: Explorer archetype requires a Value beat to satisfy their discovery mindset. At the decision funnel stage, visitors already have awareness — they need to see MODE's core capabilities and outcome promises articulated clearly to reinforce their leaning toward commitment.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Explorer archetype guidance requires Credibility. For startups at the decision stage, trust signals — customer logos, testimonials, authority markers — reduce perceived risk and validate that other innovative teams have chosen MODE.

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Decision funnel stage requires Evidence before Conversion. Concrete metrics, case study data, and proof points give the Explorer the substantive validation they need — converting curiosity into confidence without feeling pressured.

beats.name: Decision
beats.rationale: Decision funnel stage requires a Decision beat before Conversion. Pricing and plan comparison give the Explorer clarity on commitment options, framing the choice in a low-pressure way that respects their discovery-oriented mindset.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Placed immediately before Recovery per rules. For an Explorer archetype, this should be an inviting CTA — start a trial, explore a sandbox — rather than hard-sell language, honoring their no-pressure disposition while still enabling the convert-to-paid goal.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Always last. Provides secondary paths — documentation, community, contact — for Explorers who aren't ready to commit yet, ensuring no visitor hits a dead end and every exit point offers a softer re-engagement opportunity.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Establishes immediate context for startup founders exploring an AI-first, intent-driven design system. Sets the frame for what MODE is and who it serves, anchoring the Explorer archetype's need to understand before engaging further.

sections.name: Features
sections.rationale: Explorer archetype requires a Value beat to satisfy their discovery mindset. At the decision funnel stage, visitors already have awareness — they need to see MODE's core capabilities and outcome promises articulated clearly to reinforce their leaning toward commitment.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Decision-stage conversion pages require minimal variant to 'strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only' — the persistent CTA is 'the most converted element on most SaaS pages.'

Explorer archetype 'prefers editorial or with-social-proof — discovery tone, breathing room, no pressure toward conversion' and with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster for organic discovery context.

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: MODE is the semantic layer above your component library — resolving behavior, visual expression, and content register to the right experience for every archetype, funnel stage, and context. No manual variants. No QA overhead. No Figma handoff cycles.
slots.trust_signal: One-time purchase. Lifetime access. Runs in your codebase — not a vendor dashboard.

page.reasoning: Explorer variant 'uses 2-col-heavy with longer copy per feature' to support the discovery-focused narrative and deeper engagement appropriate for organic, intent-aware audiences.

slots.headline: Everything the resolution layer needs to ship.
slots.subhead: MODE gives your design system a decision model — not more configuration. Here is what that looks like in practice.

features.title: Semantic resolution engine
features.description: Components resolve behavior across four dimensions simultaneously — archetype, funnel stage, intent state, and context mode. The system makes the right call by default, so your team stops making it manually on every build.

features.title: IA planner + multi-variant generation
features.description: Feed the agent a brief and receive a full page architecture in seconds, with Mover, Validator, and Explorer variants produced in parallel. Powered by Claude Opus via your own Anthropic API key — you own the token spend entirely.

features.title: Palette map
features.description: Visual emphasis resolves per component per funnel stage without a single manual assignment. Accent weight, hierarchy, and contrast accumulate semantically toward conversion — the system holds the logic so your palette stays coherent at scale.

features.title: Behavioral tokens
features.description: Copy density, evidence requirements, and CTA rules are all driven by archetype tokens, not gut feel. The Explorer gets depth and optionality; the Mover gets directness and momentum. The token layer enforces that distinction everywhere it matters.

features.title: Component manifest
features.description: Eleven components fully specified with zone affinity, slot schemas, and usage intent — built on Radix UI primitives, so there is nothing new to learn. Extend the manifest, swap a preset, or fork the whole thing. It is infrastructure you control.

features.title: Token dashboard + Next.js preview
features.description: Edit your palette map, switch presets, and reconfigure accent rules from a single token dashboard. The Next.js preview UI renders agent output instantly with palette and label overlays — so you see the intelligence working before you ship a line.

page.reasoning: Decision-stage conversion intent requires 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows.'
